setting.note: An American-born Chinese economics professor flies to Singapore with her boyfriend and discovers that his family is impossibly wealthy. The map runs from the arrivals hall at Changi across the island: a hidden colonial estate near the Botanic Gardens, the hawker stalls of Newton, the shops of Orchard Road, the rooftop of Marina Bay Sands and the beaches of Sentosa.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Kevin Kwan, first published 2013. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Crazy Rich Asians, Kevin Kwan (2013). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The old-money gold of a family that never talks about its wealth. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
